claim_text: NVIDIA announced Cosmos world models, Isaac simulation frameworks and Isaac GR00T N models to support production-scale physical AI and robotics partners.
notes: Strong platform/enabler evidence; not evidence of material humanoid revenue.

claim_text: NVIDIA research describes Isaac GR00T N1 as a generalist robot model trained on real-robot trajectories, human videos and synthetic data.
notes: Product evidence for the foundation-model layer, but not monetization evidence.

claim_text: Tesla Q1 2026 investor materials list Optimus under robotics manufacturing capacity with California and Texas status shown as construction.
notes: Official IR source path; exact PDF path should be archived before external publication.

claim_text: Teradyne's 2025 10-K identifies Robotics as a reportable segment and discloses restructuring charges related to Robotics.
notes: Commercial robotics exposure exists, but this is not humanoid-specific.

claim_text: Symbotic's FY2025 10-K describes robotic warehouse automation systems and disclosed approximately $22.5B of backlog as of September 27 2025.
notes: Real automation revenue/backlog, but not humanoid-specific; backlog quality and customer concentration matter.

claim_text: TrendForce 2026 humanoid robot bulletin described Tesla Optimus mass-production-line targets and market validation phase.
notes: Useful market-heat context; should not drive a single-stock conclusion.

claim_text: The public-market humanoid robotics theme should route to industry_map_first because pure direct humanoid value capture is mostly private or pre-revenue.
notes: Judgment depends on current public-company disclosures and should refresh if any company discloses material humanoid revenue or backlog.
